notes: STRUCTURAL placeholder seeded at Author time from this skill's outputs (Charter refines into graded JTBD evals with a media-judge vision dimension later). rm-audioviz is a capability skill woven into rm-build: it stages the audio to WAV, guarantees the bundled Spectrum.tsx is present, and composes the visualizer — it owns no phase/numbered artifact and does NOT render. These checks verify the bundled component + script exist and the wiring is shaped correctly; they are not regex test logic.

expectations.desc: The vetted component exists at remotion-project/src/components/Spectrum.tsx (bundled in the starter, copied by init.sh) and exports `Spectrum` + `getBassIntensity`, importing visualizeAudio / useWindowedAudioData from @remotion/media-utils.

When a non-WAV bed is used, scripts/stage-audio.sh produced public/audio/<name>.wav and printed JSON with static_path + window_in_seconds + spectrum_component:true; a voiceover source uses public/voiceover/narration.wav with no transcode.

<Spectrum> is composed into the active composition with a staticFile() WAV src, an on-brand color/gradient, a power-of-2 numberOfSamples, inside <SafeZone>; no Math.random / Date.now / setTimeout / CSS transition introduced (passes rm-validate lint + tsc).
With no audio source the visualizer is skipped (or a frame-driven placeholder used) and the fallback is recorded (state.md / 06-summary.md audio_reactive:false) — no user prompt, no fabricated audio.
